dependencies.description:
app-model is a generic application schema implemented in python, for
declarative organization of application data, such as menus,
keybindings, actions/commands, etc...
dependencies.name: py-app-model

dependencies.description:
A small Python module for determining appropriate platform-specific
dirs, e.g. a "user data dir".
dependencies.name: py-appdirs

dependencies.description: Cachey is a package for caching of analytic computations.
dependencies.name: py-cachey

dependencies.description:
Certifi: A carefully curated collection of Root Certificates for
validating the trustworthiness of SSL certificates while verifying the
identity of TLS hosts.
dependencies.name: py-certifi

dependencies.description: Dask is a flexible parallel computing library for analytics.
dependencies.name: py-dask

dependencies.description:
Python library for reading and writing image data. Imageio is a Python
library that provides an easy interface to read and write a wide range
of image data, including animated images, video, volumetric data, and
scientific formats. It is cross-platform, runs on Python 2.7 and 3.4+,
and is easy to install.
dependencies.name: py-imageio

dependencies.description: Jsonschema: An(other) implementation of JSON Schema for Python.
dependencies.name: py-jsonschema

dependencies.description: lazy_loader makes it easy to load subpackages and functions on demand.
dependencies.name: py-lazy-loader

dependencies.description:
magicgui is a python library for building graphical user interfaces
(GUIs). It aims to speed up data workflows by providing a simple,
consistent API for creating GUIs to control various data types, that
work across various environments.
dependencies.name: py-magicgui

dependencies.description: This napari plugin adds a console to napari
dependencies.name: py-napari-console

dependencies.description:
napari-plugin-engine is a fork of pluggy modified by the napari team for
use in napari.
dependencies.name: py-napari-plugin-engine

dependencies.description:
The napari plugin manager provides a graphical user interface for
installing napari plugins.
dependencies.name: py-napari-plugin-manager

dependencies.description: A plugin for writing svg files with napari.
dependencies.name: py-napari-svg

dependencies.description:
The napari plugin engine version 2, npe2 extends the functionality of
napari's core. The plugin ecosystem offers user additional functionality
for napari as well as specific support for different scientific domains.
dependencies.name: py-npe2

dependencies.description: Fundamental package for array computing in Python.
dependencies.name: py-numpy

dependencies.description: numpydoc - Numpy's Sphinx extensions
dependencies.name: py-numpydoc

dependencies.description:
pandas is a fast, powerful, flexible and easy to use open source data
analysis and manipulation tool, built on top of the Python programming
language.
dependencies.name: py-pandas

dependencies.description:
Pillow is a fork of the Python Imaging Library (PIL). It adds image
processing capabilities to your Python interpreter. This library
supports many file formats, and provides powerful image processing and
graphics capabilities.
dependencies.name: py-pillow

dependencies.description:
Pint is a Python package to define, operate and manipulate physical
quantities: the product of a numerical value and a unit of measurement.
It allows arithmetic operations between them and conversions from and to
different units.
dependencies.name: py-pint

dependencies.description: The PyPA recommended tool for installing Python packages.
dependencies.name: py-pip

dependencies.description:
psutil is a cross-platform library for retrieving information on running
processes and system utilization (CPU, memory, disks, network) in
Python.
dependencies.name: py-psutil

dependencies.description: Fast python callback/event system modeled after Qt Signals.
dependencies.name: py-psygnal

dependencies.description: Data validation and settings management using Python type hinting.
dependencies.name: py-pydantic

dependencies.description: Pygments is a syntax highlighting package written in Python.
dependencies.name: py-pygments

dependencies.description:
PyOpenGL is the most common cross platform Python binding to OpenGL and
related APIs.
dependencies.name: py-pyopengl

dependencies.description:
PyQt is a set of Python v2 and v3 bindings for The Qt Company's Qt
application framework and runs on all platforms supported by Qt
including Windows, OS X, Linux, iOS and Android. PyQt5 supports Qt v5.
dependencies.name: py-pyqt5

dependencies.description: PyYAML is a YAML parser and emitter for Python.
dependencies.name: py-pyyaml

dependencies.description: QtPy: Abtraction layer for PyQt5/PyQt4/PySide/PySide2
dependencies.name: py-qtpy

dependencies.description:
Image processing algorithms for SciPy, including IO, morphology,
filtering, warping, color manipulation, object detection, etc.

dependencies.description: Fundamental algorithms for scientific computing in Python.

well as experimental types that will eventually be added to the typing
module, such as Protocol (see PEP 544 for details about protocols and
static duck typing).

dependencies.description:
VisPy is a high-performance interactive 2D/3D data visualization
library. VisPy leverages the computational power of modern Graphics
Processing Units (GPUs) through the OpenGL library to display very large
datasets.
dependencies.name: py-vispy

dependencies.description: A built-package format for Python.

dependencies.description: The Python programming language.
dependencies.name: python

dependencies.description: A Spack managed Python virtual environment
dependencies.name: python-venv

dependent_to.description:
This is a helper package for relion, not to be used by end-users. relion
(for REgularised LIkelihood OptimisatioN, pronounce rely-on) is a
software package that employs an empirical Bayesian approach for
electron cryo-microscopy (cryo-EM) structure determination.
dependent_to.name: py-relion

description:
napari is a fast, interactive, multi-dimensional image viewer for
Python. It's designed for browsing, annotating, and analyzing large
multi-dimensional images. It's built on top of Qt (for the GUI), vispy
(for performant GPU-based rendering), and the scientific Python stack
(numpy, scipy).
name: py-napari

variants.description: Build systems supported by the package
variants.name: build_system
